channel.title: theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from theBalancedBriefNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Eleven-year Alaska analysis finds no spike in traumatic injury or death after cash transfers
item.description: Cash transfer programs, which provide money directly to recipients, are growing in the United States, but face significant scrutiny, with questions ov...

item.title: New artificial intelligence tool diagnoses masked hypertension
item.description: About 10% of people with hypertension have normal blood pressure readings at the doctor's office. Now researchers at the University of Arkansas have d...

item.title: In comparing real-world costs and benefits of fibromyalgia medications, a clear winner emerges
item.description: Fibromyalgia is a long-term chronic condition that causes widespread pain in the muscles and soft tissues throughout the body. The illness can affect ...

item.title: RHR: Vitamin D: Why a Personalized Approach Is Best
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris Kresser explains why vitamin D supplementation should never be one-size-fits-all. Drawing on new res...

item.title: Early-life indoor mold linked to reduced childhood lung function, long-term study finds
item.description: Mold is a silent threat, often going unnoticed as it quietly harms health. What's concerning is that exposure to mold during early childhood leaves it...

item.title: Mindfulness enables more effective endoscopies in awake patients, study finds
item.description: A new study has shown that mindfulness helps patients to relax during an endoscopy, allowing doctors to carry out detailed examinations without the ne...

item.title: RHR: Cold Exposure: Why Sustained Beats Extreme
item.description: Cold plunges and ice baths dominate social media, but do they really deliver the best metabolic benefits? In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, ...

item.title: RHR: Why Your Sleep Schedule Matters As Much As Duration

item.title: RHR: Menopause Brain Fog—What’s Really Happening (and How To Fix It)
item.description: In this episode of Revolution Health Radio, Chris examines research confirming that menopause-related brain fog is real, measurable, and reversible. H...

item.title: RHR: Seed Oils and Colon Cancer – What the New Research Really Shows
